prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein were computed with chemkit’s fukui-reactivity skill using DFT at tier ‘standard’ (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, no density fitting) in PySCF v?. The calculation was a single-point evaluation on the user-supplied geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz); no further optimisation was performed, so the property is B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input.
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent). Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet). Both N – 1 and N + 1 states were treated as doublets (multiplicity 2). The SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; the engine’s integrity.trustworthy flag is true.
Live run-time output was streamed to the default chemkit log (<subcommand>_<timestamp>.out) in the working directory; the full JSON results are in acrolein_fukui_dft.json, with the CLI invocation recorded inside.

Selected numerical results (Mulliken scheme, values in e):
• f⁺ (electrophilic attack, per atom): [0.40136419, 0.022255105, 0.43404673, 0.64232102, –0.1572595, –0.2072029, –0.062116996, –0.073407645]
• f⁻ (nucleophilic attack, per atom): [0.39388616, 0.0059884352, 0.091403097, 0.087369815, 0.085961423, 0.1856796, 0.084485809, 0.065225658]
• Dual descriptor (f⁺ – f⁻): [0.0074780351, 0.01626667, 0.34264363, 0.5549512, –0.24322092, –0.39288251, –0.14660281, –0.1386333]

Most electrophilic site: C4 (index 3) with f⁺ = 0.64232102.
Most nucleophilic site: O1 (index 0) with f⁻ = 0.39388616.

Verbatim warnings from chemkit:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
